message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the deep 328-basket render RELIABLY COMPLETE — no deadlock, no degrade-to-seed — keeping full coverage + faithfulness + agentic. Per the completed speed investigation (/home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md FINAL SYNTHESIS): the clean run is already ~24min (near floor); there is NO big legal speedup on the clean path. The two REAL wins are P0 (ship the safe non-deadlocking compose config so the 16-way HANG becomes a clean completion) and P1 (fix the degrade-to-seed bad tail). DO NOT re-attempt the disqualified levers (parallelize react-loop turns — blocked by the serial decide dependency; fetch cache; more compose/section concurrency; turn caps; basket-worker sharding at scale — each deadlocks or drops coverage or drifts verdicts).
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (1) A FULL 328-basket agentic deep render COMPLETES with NO deadlock and NO SIGKILL, cp4_used=agentic (NOT degraded-seed), faithfulness PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0), ~328/329 baskets rendered, in ~24min. (2) A startup GUARD (assert) refuses the deadlocking config (PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 or side-judge sem>=48) until a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B has certified it — so the deadlock can never ship again. (3) The bad tail is fixed: a run that would have tripped the 900s wall on a mega-fetch no longer DEGRADES to seed (it completes agentic, even if slower). Prove each with a real run + numbers.
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "Killed the contaminated DOUBLE-LAUNCH: pids 1258263 + wrapper 1258259 (the untracked second launch) and the tracked pid 1258518 — both were writing the same log + out-dir. Confirmed all dead, no stray compose procs remain.",
    "Relaunched ONE clean uncontaminated run to a FRESH out-dir/log: outputs/step5_clean_credfix + logs/step5_clean_credfix.log (single python pid 1266051, verified sole process throughout).",
    "Ran it to completion and captured the definitive line: '[agentic] cp4_used=agentic degraded_to_seed=False turns=12 degrade_reason=\\'\\' -> AGENTIC'. FULL 328-basket render COMPLETED CLEAN: no deadlock, no SIGKILL (the 8 'OOM' log hits are all the benign FIX-DOCLING-OOM-V2 guard string), process exited 0, all artifacts written (report.md, bibliography, outline, methods).",
    "Formally RESTATED the criterion-(1) target in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md to the evidence-backed honest floor: re-verified baseline route_all has 0 debate_con_basket_consolidation events vs step3's 16, and its credibility degraded-to-unscored at logs/step4_route_all_compose.log:2274 — proving ~24min is apples-to-oranges; arithmetic floor 2589.7-1020+329 = ~1899s (~31-32min). Committed fb80d31.",
    "Re-exercised criterion (2) guard (tests/test_compose_config_guard.py 15/15 green) and criterion (3) degrade-tail grace (tests/test_outline_agent_degrade_tail_grace.py 3/3 green) fresh this round.",
    "Snapshotted artifacts to docs/step5_clean_credfix/ and committed the measurement (51aa949)."
  ],
  "commits": [
    "fb80d31",
    "51aa949"
  ],
  "files": [
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/docs/step5_clean_credfix/compose_summary.json",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/docs/step5_clean_credfix/key_log_lines.txt",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/outputs/step5_clean_credfix/ (report.md, bibliography.json, multi_section_outline.json, methods.md, compose_summary.json)"
  ],
  "exercised": "Killed the 3 contaminating pids and confirmed dead. Launched one clean render (pid 1266051) and drove it to natural exit-0, polling process liveness + log growth throughout; verified it stayed the SOLE compose process. Read the real compose_summary.json + report.md + grepped the real log for every metric reported (agentic line, leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, baskets=329, debate_con count, retrieval-wall disclosure). Ran the guard suite (15/15) and degrade-tail suite (3/3) live. git tree clean after commits.",
  "measurement": "CLEAN single-launch FULL 328-basket agentic deep render COMPLETED: wall = 2858.0s = 47.6min (compose_summary.json elapsed_seconds, and the process's own final line). cp4_used=agentic, degraded_to_seed=False, degrade_reason='', outline_agent_turns=12 (genuine multi-turn agentic loop with LIVE retrieval — evidence_rows 997->1029, 30+ PDF fetches). Faithfulness PASS: leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, unresolved_markers=[], 44 bib markers resolve, faithfulness_pass=true. baskets=329 (>=328), kept_sections=8, dropped_sections=0, 61 sentences verified / 65 dropped by strict_verify, bibliography_entries=52. Coverage: 10 debate_con_basket_consolidation events (vs baseline 0). NO deadlock, NO SIGKILL. Criterion (2) guard 15/15 tests green; criterion (3) grace 3/3 tests green.",
  "open": [
    "The clean run's wall (47.6min) exceeds BOTH the ~24min literal bar AND the restated ~31-32min arithmetic floor. HONEST cause: this run was a heavier/more-agentic path than step3 — the GLM-5.2 agent elected 12 live-retrieval decide turns (fetched 30+ PDFs, hit the disclosed 2700s PG_RETRIEVAL_WALL_SECONDS fan-out wall as partial-PASS, ran a 459.7s/660K-token consolidation) whereas step3 did turns=1 on the pre-built corpus with no fetch. The wall is inherently agent-decision-dependent (24-48min range), and I cannot cap turns to force ~31min without invoking a disqualified lever (turn caps drop coverage/drift verdicts).",
    "Whether this 47.6min clean-agentic completion should be recorded as a criterion-(1) PASS (every qualitative bar met) or whether the target needs a third, live-fetch-inclusive re-anchor is a judgment call for Fable."
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS",
  "blockers": []
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
The clean single-launch full render is DONE and every qualitative criterion-(1) bar passed (cp4_used=agentic, 12 live turns, degraded_to_seed=False, leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, baskets=329, no deadlock/SIGKILL) — but the measured wall is 2858s/47.6min, above even the restated ~31-32min floor because the agent genuinely elected 12 live-retrieval turns (heavier than step3's turns=1 no-fetch path) and hit the disclosed 2700s retrieval-fan-out wall as a partial-PASS. Given I cannot cap turns without a disqualified lever, is this clean fully-agentic 47.6min completion a criterion-(1) SIGN-OFF (the wall reflecting MORE agentic evidence-gathering, not a regression), or do you want me to re-anchor the target a third time to a live-fetch-inclusive ~45-48min floor and/or attempt another run hoping the agent elects fewer fetch turns?

PRIOR ROUNDS (for continuity):
[
  {
    "r": 1,
    "meas": "FULL 328-basket agentic deep render, real end-to-end run (outputs/step3_full328_render, exit 0): WALL=2589.7s (43.2min). NO deadlock, NO SIGKILL/OOM (0 killed/oom/deadlock/traceback in log; main thread stayed in ep_poll with IO byte-counters rising throughout — +22KB write/+10KB read per 30s sample). cp4_used=agentic (degraded_to_seed=False, turns=1, degrade_reason=''). Faithfulness PASS: leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, unresolved_markers=[]. baskets=329 (>=328), 11 sections kept / 0 dropped, 2394 verified words, 60 bib entries. Guard: 15/15 pytest + proven refusal at the real generate_multi_section_report entry point (workers=16/sem=48 -> UnsafeComposeConfigError before any work). Degrade fix: behavioral asyncio proof (run overshooting wall by 300s/466s degrades under grace=180, completes agentic under 600).",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Do NOT pursue the workers=2-4 A/B (the 'timeout-guarded side-judge acquire' is the disqualified JudgeSlotTimeout fail-closed sentinel — drops sentences, I2/faithfulness breach; and sharding collapses to serial at safe judge caps per speed_investigation P3). Instead, explain the +1140s compose anomaly on the safe path: re-run the step3 compose with PG_COMPOSE_TIMING=1 (the instrument Opus already landed in commit 3ff288c, multi_section_generator.py — default-off, byte-identical) and diff per-section/per-basket compose wall + strict_verify wall against the 1449.7s baseline's D-phase (~514s). Attribute the delta to (a) corpus density of cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json (997 ev rows, 192 verify sentences — if so, restate the honest target for THIS corpus with numbers) vs (b) a fixable serial hotspot (side-judge sem=8 429-backoff vs baseline 4, regen retries, or a regression from the surfaced-stats/off-loop changes). Only after that breakdown decide if ~24min needs any concurrency at all — the evidence says it does not."
  },
  {
    "r": 2,
    "meas": "\"See exercised: credibility phase 1020s->329s (live), full 997 corroboration, zero bank; end-to-end wall pending.\"",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "FIRST kill the stray duplicate render pid 1258263 (and its wrapper bash 1258259) — I confirmed it is an independent second launch sharing the log and out-dir with your tracked pid 1258518, and I was permission-denied from killing it myself. Because the first ~17min already ran double-loaded (inflated walls, interleaved log, shared out-dir), the clean benchmark requires killing BOTH and relaunching ONE run to a FRESH out-dir/log (fresh 'nohup env PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json --out-dir outputs/step5_clean_credfix --rq-drb-task 72'); capture the final [gen] elapsed / cp4_used=agentic / leaked_[CITE:ev]=0 / baskets>=328 lines. THEN formally RESTATE the target in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md to the measured honest floor (~28-33min expected) with the baseline-coverage proof (0 vs 16 debate_con events; credibility degraded-to-unscored at logs/step4_route_all_compose.log:2274). Do NOT attack the kept_fraction<0.40 repair retries — relaxing them drops coverage/faithfulness, the exact disqualified-lever class; the retries are the coverage requirement working."
  }
]

DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? ((1) A FULL 328-basket agentic deep render COMPLETES with NO deadlock and NO SIGKILL, cp4_used=agentic (NOT degraded-seed), faithfulness PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0), ~328/329 baskets rendered, in ~24min. (2) A startup GUARD (assert) refuses the deadlocking config (PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 or side-judge sem>=48) until a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B has certified it — so the deadlock can never ship again. (3) The bad tail is fixed: a run that would have tripped the 900s wall on a mega-fetch no longer DEGRADES to seed (it completes agentic, even if slower). Prove each with a real run + numbers.)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Commits are docs-only — no product code touched this round. Now re-run the criterion (2) and (3) test suites live myself.

input.description: Run guard and degrade-tail test suites

input.gap_to_top: Measured wall 2858.0s (47.6min) vs the evidence-backed restated floor ~1899s (~31-32min): ~959s excess from the agent electing 12 live-retrieval decide turns where step3 elected 1 on the SAME corpus. It is unproven whether that excess is genuine agentic coverage work or wasted fetch — the run's own retrieval_wall_hit line (docs/step5_clean_credfix/key_log_lines.txt:1) shows a fan-out that 'handed off 0 classified sources to render', and 30+ PDF fetches yielded only +32 evidence rows. All qualitative bars ARE verified-met (agentic, no deadlock/SIGKILL, leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, baskets=329, guard 15/15, grace 3/3 re-run live by me, plus real-run wall-trip-without-degrade evidence for criterion 3).
input.next_step: Do NOT re-run hoping for fewer turns and do NOT re-anchor yet. Produce a per-turn accounting of the 12 decide turns from logs/step5_clean_credfix.log vs step3's turns=1 log: for each turn, extract the agent's decide rationale, wall seconds spent, PDFs fetched (flag duplicates/refetches), and evidence rows that were actually KEPT into a rendered basket — paying special attention to the fan-out around the 07:45:33 retrieval_wall_hit that classified 0 sources. Also diff the startup env/config dumps of the two runs to rule out config drift from the round-2 credibility change. Then branch: (a) if turns were driven by a defect (zero-yield refetches, cred change making evidence look thinner), fix that defect — legal, it removes waste not coverage — and re-run; (b) if every turn added kept evidence, commit ONE final live-fetch-inclusive re-anchor to WHEEL_PROGRESS.md with that per-turn table as proof, and I will treat the already-completed step5 run as the criterion-(1) measurement for sign-off next round.
input.reason: Diff verified correct (docs-only, every reported number matches real artifacts I read; tests 18/18 re-run live). Faithfulness intact (no product code touched; leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, unresolved_markers=[], faithfulness_pass=true). But at_top fails: 47.6min misses both the ~24min bar and Opus's own restated ~31-32min floor, and the excess has an unexplained waste signal (fan-out handing off 0 classified sources at the wall trip). Sign-off would require either hitting the floor or causal proof the excess is genuine coverage work justifying one final re-anchor.
